_source: Culinary Institute textbooks, The Flavor Bible, ServSafe certification, Professional kitchen SOPs
_note: Long-tail culinary knowledge. Cooking techniques, flavor pairing, food safety, kitchen operations.

roasting.temperature: 325-450°F; lower for large cuts, higher for vegetables/small proteins
roasting.technique: Uncovered in oven; hot air circulation; browning via Maillard reaction
roasting.vegetables: 400-450°F; toss in oil; spread single layer; caramelization

grilling.direct_heat: Food directly over flame; high heat 450-550°F; steaks, burgers, vegetables
grilling.indirect_heat: Food to side of flame; lid closed; convection; large cuts, whole chickens
grilling.two_zone: Hot zone for searing; cool zone for finishing; prevents burning
grilling.resting: 5 min for steaks; 15-20 min for roasts; redistributes juices; temp rises 5-10°F

sautéing.technique: High heat; small amount fat; keep food moving; quick cooking
sautéing.pan_selection: Stainless steel (fond for sauces); nonstick (eggs, delicate fish); cast iron (heat retention)
sautéing.temperature: Medium-high to high; oil shimmers but not smoking; protein sizzles immediately
sautéing.crowding: Don't overcrowd; steams instead of browns; work in batches

pan_searing.process: Pat dry; season; hot pan; oil; protein; don't move 3-4 min; flip once
pan_searing.crust_formation: Maillard reaction 285-330°F; browning and flavor development
pan_searing.finishing: Finish in 350-400°F oven for thick cuts; ensures even doneness

braising.steps: 1) Sear protein, 2) Remove, sauté aromatics, 3) Deglaze, 4) Return protein, add liquid 1/3 up sides, 5) Cover, low heat or oven 300-325°F
braising.cuts: Tough cuts with connective tissue; chuck roast, short ribs, pork shoulder, lamb shanks
braising.liquid: Stock, wine, beer; aromatic (onion, carrot, celery, garlic, herbs)
braising.time: 2-4 hours until fork-tender; collagen → gelatin; low slow cooking essential

poaching.temperature: 160-180°F; small bubbles on bottom, no breaking surface
poaching.liquid: Court bouillon (water, wine, aromatics); milk; stock
poaching.shallow_vs_submersion: Shallow (1/2 covered) for fish; submersion for chicken

steaming.method: Boiling water below, steam cooks food above; no direct contact
steaming.advantages: Retains nutrients, moisture, shape; no added fat
steaming.vegetables: 3-7 min for most; bright color, slight crunch

stir_frying.wok_technique: Highest heat; constant motion; cook in order (longest first); add sauce last
stir_frying.mise_en_place: Everything prepped before starting; uniform sizes for even cooking
stir_frying.order: Aromatics (garlic, ginger) 30 sec → protein → hard vegetables → soft vegetables → sauce
stir_frying.cornstarch_slurry: 1:1 cornstarch:water; add at end; thickens sauce; stir constantly

cuts.brunoise: 1/8" × 1/8" × 1/8" dice; julienne then dice; fine dice for garnish
cuts.chiffonade: Thin ribbons; stack leaves, roll, slice; basil, spinach, herbs
cuts.mince: Finest cut; garlic, ginger, herbs; nearly paste

technique.claw_grip: Fingers curled under; knuckles guide blade; fingertips safe
technique.rocking_motion: Tip stays on board; heel rocks up/down; forward motion; efficient
technique.sharpening: Honing steel realigns; whetstone sharpens; 15-20° angle; weekly honing, monthly stone

five_tastes.sweet: Sugars; honey, fruits, caramelization; balances bitter, sour, spicy
five_tastes.salty: Salt, soy sauce, fish sauce, parmesan; enhances other flavors; essential
five_tastes.sour: Acids; lemon, vinegar, tomatoes, yogurt; brightens, balances richness
five_tastes.bitter: Coffee, dark chocolate, kale, radicchio; complexity; balance with sweet/fat
five_tastes.umami: Glutamates; mushrooms, tomatoes, soy sauce, parmesan, miso, fish sauce; savory depth

balancing_flavors.too_salty: Add acid (lemon, vinegar), fat (cream, butter), or dilute (more liquid, bulk)
balancing_flavors.too_sweet: Add acid (vinegar, citrus), salt, or bitter (greens, zest)
balancing_flavors.too_sour: Add fat (cream, oil), sweet (sugar, honey), or salt
balancing_flavors.too_spicy: Add fat (dairy, coconut milk), sweet (sugar, honey), or acid (lime)
balancing_flavors.bland: Salt first, then acid (lemon, vinegar), then fat

classic_pairings.lemon_fish: Acid cuts fat; brightens delicate flavor
classic_pairings.pork_apple: Sweet fruit balances rich savory; classic combination
classic_pairings.strawberry_balsamic: Sweet fruit + aged acid; surprising synergy

building_complexity.layers: Add ingredients at different stages; raw garlic vs roasted vs toasted
building_complexity.textural_contrast: Crispy + creamy; crunchy + soft; fried shallots on soup
building_complexity.temperature_contrast: Warm protein + cold salsa; ice cream on warm pie
building_complexity.fresh_finish: Add fresh herbs, citrus zest, drizzle of oil at end; brightness

béchamel.ratio: 3 oz butter, 3 oz flour (roux) per quart milk
béchamel.technique: Cook roux 2-3 min (blonde); add warm milk gradually; whisk constantly; simmer 20 min

velouté.ratio: 3 oz butter, 3 oz flour per quart stock
velouté.derivatives: Allemande (+cream, egg yolk), Suprême (+cream), Bercy (+shallots, white wine)

espagnole.base: Roux + brown stock + tomato; brown sauce
espagnole.technique: Brown roux; add stock; add tomato paste; mirepoix; simmer 2-3 hours; strain
espagnole.derivatives: Demi-glace (+ equal brown stock, reduced), Bordelaise (+red wine, shallots, marrow)

tomato.technique: Sauté mirepoix; add tomatoes, stock, herbs; simmer 1 hour; strain or purée

hollandaise.ratio: 3 egg yolks, 1 lb clarified butter, 2 tbsp lemon juice
hollandaise.technique: Whisk yolks + water in double boiler; slowly drizzle butter while whisking; 180°F max
hollandaise.derivatives: Béarnaise (+tarragon, shallots, white wine), Choron (+tomato paste)
hollandaise.broken_sauce: Too hot (scrambled); too cold (separated); fix: new yolk + slowly add broken sauce

food_safety.temperature_danger_zone: 41-135°F; bacteria multiply rapidly; keep food out of zone

cooking_temperatures.whole_cuts: 145°F + 3 min rest (beef, pork, lamb, veal); 145°F fish
cooking_temperatures.eggs: 160°F for egg dishes; 145°F if served immediately

cooling_food.two_stage: 135°F to 70°F within 2 hours; 70°F to 41°F within 4 hours; total 6 hours max
cooling_food.methods: Ice bath; shallow pans (<2" deep); blast chiller; separate large batches

cross_contamination.color_coded_boards: Red = raw meat; yellow = poultry; blue = fish; green = produce; white = dairy/bread
cross_contamination.separate_storage: Raw below cooked; lowest shelf for raw meat; wrapped to prevent drips
cross_contamination.clean_sanitize: 1) Scrape, 2) Wash (soap, hot water), 3) Rinse, 4) Sanitize (bleach solution or commercial), 5) Air dry

allergens.big_8: Milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans (90% of reactions)
allergens.prevention: Separate storage; dedicated equipment; clear labeling; train staff; inform customers

white_stock.ingredients: Bones (chicken, veal, fish) + mirepoix (onion, carrot, celery 2:1:1) + bouquet garni (bay, thyme, parsley stems, peppercorns)
white_stock.process: Cold water; bring to simmer; skim impurities; simmer 3-4 hours chicken, 6-8 hours veal, 45 min fish; strain

brown_stock.roasting: Roast bones 400°F until browned; roast mirepoix; adds color and depth
brown_stock.tomato_paste: Adds color, umami, body; 2-3 tbsp per gallon
brown_stock.deglazing: Deglaze roasting pan; add to stock; captures fond

stocks_and_broths.ratios: 1 lb bones per quart water (approx); adjust for concentration

leavening.yeast: Biological; fermentation produces CO₂; bread, pizza dough; requires time (rise)
leavening.baking_powder: Chemical; double-acting (first in liquid, second in heat); quick breads, cakes
leavening.baking_soda: Requires acid (buttermilk, vinegar, cocoa); single-acting; immediate reaction

gluten_development.formation: Gluten proteins (glutenin + gliadin) + water + agitation = elastic network
gluten_development.desired_low: Pie crust, biscuits; minimal mixing; butter coats proteins; flaky, tender
gluten_development.inhibitors: Fat (shortening, butter); sugar; acid; coat proteins, prevent gluten formation
